text: Meet Stan, he had all day to go for one run. He didn't, and now he feels like a failure. But Stan isn't lazy. His brain is a million year old survival machine, built to save energy, and it's just doing its job. So why does doing nothing feel like a sin? That part is way weirder than you think.

segments.text: Meet Stan, he had all day to go for one run.

built to save energy, and it's just doing its job.

That part is way weirder than you think.
